summary: Consolidated GitHub Actions workflows by renaming `.github/workflows/build-melee.yml` to `.github/workflows/build.yml` and folding the separate `publish-pages.yml` workflow into it. The unified workflow now runs on push and pull_request, builds Melee, generates GitHub Pages output with the existing `gen-pages` container image, uploads the pages artifact, and deploys only from the default branch.
what_changed: Changed workflow metadata from `name: build-melee` to `name: build` and from `run-name: Build Melee` to `run-name: Build Melee & publish GitHub Pages`.
Changed the workflow trigger from reusable-only `workflow_call` to direct `push` and `pull_request` triggers.
Added Pages-related workflow permissions: `contents: read`, `pages: write`, and `id-token: write`.
Moved the `gen-pages` job from `publish-pages.yml` into the main build workflow, with `needs: build-ninja`.
Moved the `deploy-pages` job into the main build workflow, preserving default-branch-only deployment with `if: github.ref_name == github.event.repository.default_branch`.

Kept the page generation path using `ghcr.io`, `doldecomp/melee/gen-pages:latest`, `actions/checkout@v3`, `docker/login-action@v2`, `actions/configure-pages@v4`, `actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3`, and `actions/deploy-pages@v4`.
smart_moves: Eliminated an extra workflow indirection: the deleted `publish-pages.yml` previously called `./.github/workflows/build-melee.yml` as a reusable workflow before generating pages.
Kept Pages deployment gated to the repository default branch, allowing the workflow to run for pull requests without deploying PR output.
Preserved separate generation and deployment jobs, with deployment depending on successful page generation.
Used explicit GitHub Pages permissions instead of broader repository permissions.
Used a `concurrency` group for deployment with `cancel-in-progress: false`, reducing risk of overlapping Pages deployments.
decomp_lessons: CI workflow consolidation can reduce maintenance burden when one workflow exists mainly to wrap another plus a small post-build step.
For decomp projects with generated progress pages, tying page generation directly to the build workflow helps ensure published artifacts reflect the same successful build pipeline.
Default-branch checks are important when workflows run on both `push` and `pull_request` but should only publish from canonical repository state.
No C decompilation, symbol matching, struct layout, or source/header organization changes were present in this PR.
naming_conventions: Renamed the workflow file/name from the more specific `build-melee` to the shorter `build`.
Kept human-readable job names such as `Make`, `Generate pages`, and `Deploy to GitHub Pages`.
The page generator image is assembled from `IMAGE_REPO=doldecomp/melee` and `IMAGE_SUFFIX=gen-pages:latest`, producing an `IMAGE` environment value used with `ghcr.io`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Not applicable; this PR only changed GitHub Actions workflow YAML and did not include assembly matching or decompilation tactics.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching `.github/workflows/build.yml` to see whether the combined build and Pages workflow caused permission, trigger, or deployment issues.
Search for changes to the `gen-pages` container image or GitHub Pages output format after this workflow consolidation.
Check whether any later CI refactors reintroduced reusable workflows or split Pages deployment back out.
